recommendations.description: Add workspace/monorepo detection enhancement for future stories (TL-001 limitation)
recommendations.rationale: Current implementation detects projects at root level. Workspace members require user specification via AskUserQuestion fallback.
recommendations.related_story: Future story for expanded monorepo support

recommendations.description: Expand ecosystem coverage to Go, Java, C# (TL-002 limitation)
recommendations.rationale: Initial implementation covers 3 ecosystems. Additional ecosystems fall back to AskUserQuestion.

performance.notes: Phase 0.3 classification uses Read() and Glob() only (no Bash build command execution), meeting performance requirements

observations.note: Phase 0.3 insertion demonstrates excellent separation of concerns. Project type detection (Phase 0.3) cleanly separates from build execution (Phase 0.2) and deployment phases (1-7). Uses marker protocol from STORY-497 as enforcement boundary.

Dual-path synchronization verified: .claude/ (operational) and src/claude/ (source mirror) contain identical Phase 0.3 content across all 753 lines. Both files properly updated during STORY-498 implementation.

Test suite comprehensively validates all 5 acceptance criteria with 30 passing tests across 5 test files. Coverage includes happy path (all 3 ecosystem types), edge cases (hybrid projects, missing config), and error handling (AskUserQuestion fallback).

Phase 0.3 correctly depends on STORY-497 (phase marker protocol). Skipped phases use marker protocol introduced in STORY-497, creating proper dependency chain: STORY-497 (protocol definition) -> STORY-498 (adaptive path) -> STORY-499 (halt trigger expansion).

Phase 0.3 contributes to defense-in-depth strategy against unintended phase skipping (RCA-041). Strategy spans: STORY-497 (runtime enforcement), STORY-498 (documented adaptive path), STORY-499 (halt trigger expansion). Library crate path is now documented, auditable, and machine-enforced.
